preamble: Vowel fronting (Umlaut or i-mutation) shifts the high back rounded vowel [u] to its high front rounded counterpart [y]. This process is typically triggered by a high front vowel or semi-vowel in the following syllable, pulling the tongue position forward while maintaining lip rounding.

examples.note: Historical i-mutation of the plural suffix caused fronting of the root vowel.
Latin long /u:/ underwent spontaneous fronting to /y/ in the development of French.

sources: Campbell, L. (2013). Historical Linguistics: An Introduction.
Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Hock, H. H. (1991). Principles of Historical Linguistics.
